pattern: Maintaining the smallest possible tail for every subsequence length lets each element replace the first tail not less than it via binary search, so the array of tails grows to exactly the longest strictly increasing subsequence length.
hints: A quadratic dynamic program asks, for each element, the longest increasing run ending there; speeding it up needs a smarter structure.
Keep a list where the entry at position k is the smallest possible ending value of an increasing subsequence of length k+1.
For each value, binary search that tails list for the leftmost element not less than it: replace it, or append if the value is larger than every tail; the final list length is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: classic mixed array with LIS length four
duplicates do not count, strictly increasing only
all equal, longest strictly increasing run is one

stress.description: random array over a moderate value range so duplicates and long runs both appear
